hypothesis: Introducing three-dimensional surface roughness and time-dependent surface chemistry into the coarse-grained polymer–surface simulations will reveal a coupling between polymer sequence motifs and local surface curvature and dynamic patches such that optimal adhesion requires alignment of specific strong-bead motifs to high-curvature, dynamically active regions; this coupling will produce PMF profiles that cannot be predicted by surface composition alone and will enable robust inverse design across rough, heterogeneous surfaces.
